examples: Where Footsteps Collide: A Spatiotemporal Model of Stair Wear
Cracking the Cyber Puzzle: A Data-Driven Framework for...

length_and_visual.visual_goal: Fill one full summary page (leave 1/4 for title/keywords)

o_award_checklist: Explicit mentions of confidence intervals or sensitivity

modeling_flow: Problem Analysis -> Flowchart -> Task-based Execution -> Model Chain/Interface

phrases: The selected value is proved to be reasonable...
The prediction aligns well with the actual results...
The model demonstrates commendable sensitivity and robustness...

weakness_to_strength.Complexity: Frame as a trade-off for high precision
weakness_to_strength.Assumptions: Frame as 'idealized abstractions' for policy speed

self_critique_framing.future_work: Directing future iterations with granular data or regional linkage

prompt_engine.title_prompt: You are an O-Award title designer. Use [Metaphor]:[Technical]. Sound intriguing yet scientific.
prompt_engine.figure_prompt: Output 1-3 figures for ONE model. Include: Title (EN+ZH), Purpose, Data Schema, Python Code, Caption Template.
prompt_engine.abstract_prompt: Write a 300-500 word narrative. No bullets. Include 3-5 numbers. Structure: Context -> Sub-tasks -> Validation -> Policy.
prompt_engine.model_section_prompt: Describe modeling framework. Must name models (e.g., DRD-CE). Use solution flowcharts. Cite classical theory.
prompt_engine.validation_prompt: Defend scientific credibility. Use ±10% perturbation. Include statistical metrics (R², AIC/BIC).
prompt_engine.critique_prompt: Critique without killing credibility. Use strengths-weaknesses parallel points. Pair every weakness with a future fix.
